content: HCV diagnosis algorithm: 1) Anti-HCV antibody screening (EIA); 2) If reactive, reflex to HCV RNA PCR to confirm active infection (antibody indicates exposure only - can be false positive or resolved infection). HCV RNA quantification establishes baseline viral load. Genotype testing needed only if using genotype-specific regimens. Liver disease staging: non-invasive methods preferred (FIB-4, APRI, FibroScan/elastography); biopsy rarely needed. ALT may be normal despite significant fibrosis. Spontaneous clearance occurs in 15-25% of acute infections (more likely symptomatic, IL28B CC genotype). Reinfection possible after SVR; ongoing risk reduction counseling essential. HCV core antigen assay alternative where RNA testing unavailable.
